meta.setting: Mountain stage road outside French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in stage travel and road danger

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Mining-town commerce, stage road texture, and northern California travel context.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Gold rush settlement, road movement, and mining-community pressure.

image_prompt.subject: a stage driver hauling a six-horse coach around a muddy mountain bend while an armed guard braces beside the luggage rack
image_prompt.scene: road after rain; wheel spray, passengers gripping windows, pines and shale slope crowding the grade, a mail sack tied high, and the next bend hidden by brush
image_prompt.composition: square action plate with the coach sweeping diagonally through frame, all horses fully visible, driver hands central, and road danger ahead rather than behind
image_prompt.camera: low roadside angle with modest motion blur, like a PS3-era open-world chase moment frozen just before the decision
material_cues: painted coach panels worn at corners
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the driver look more worried about the next bend than the visible gun.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine road action plate
render_traits: slightly simplified coach, horse, and rider silhouettes
modest texture resolution on mud, leather, wood, paint, and shale
hard ambient occlusion under coach, harness, wheels, and horse bodies
baked storm-after-rain light with wet highlights
believable 1899 stagecoach, clothing, tack, and mail gear
kinetic travel pressure without modern cinematic gloss
style_modifiers.palette: mud umber, wet pine green, coach red-brown, horsehide brown, oilcloth black, brass dull gold, storm gray, and shale blue.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable coach names, mail labels, signs, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos
cropped horse team or cropped coach

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The stagecoach, full horse team, driver, guard, luggage, and muddy bend are readable.
Every visible prop and garment belongs plausibly to 1899 stage travel.
The image shows route danger and schedule pressure, not decorative nostalgia.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
